concerns.text: FR-005 specifies using a cluster-based FWER correction via Threshold-Free Cluster Enhancement (TFCE) as the primary gate for identifying significant clusters in node-level centrality metrics. TFCE is designed for spatially contiguous data (e.g., voxelwise neuroimaging) and assumes a notion of spatial adjacency, which is not naturally defined for graph nodes derived from functional connectivity matrices. Applying TFCE to isolated node-wise statistics may be methodologically inappropriate and could invalidate the correction procedure. Consider using a multiple-comparison correction method suited to independent node-level tests (e.g., permutation-based max‑t, Bonferroni, or FDR alone) or explicitly define a spatial adjacency structure for nodes if TFCE is to be retained.
